steps.title: Find the orbital difference
steps.content: The student wants to contrast the two moons' orbits. The notes give one clear difference: Callisto takes 16.69 Earth days to orbit Jupiter, while Adrastea takes only 0.30 Earth days.

steps.title: Reject similarities and errors
steps.content: B and C emphasize similarities involving Kepler's laws rather than contrasting the moons. D invents a circular orbit for Adrastea, even though the notes connect moons with elliptical orbits.

steps.title: Compare orbital speeds
steps.content: Adrastea completes its orbit many times faster than Callisto, directly contrasting their orbital periods. The answer is A.
